title: DRAM2Utils --input_genes id-remap + run-hardening

decisions.input_strategy: A (rename emitted ids + reverse-map query_id->locus_id inside DRAM2Utils); B (--input_fasta) rejected as incompatible with GAA seq-hash cross-genome batching
decisions.emitted_id_scheme: g_<n> (1-based per-batch integer); store locus_id never written into the FASTA header
decisions.env_ownership: config keys dram2.nxf_ver (default 24.10.5) + dram2.env_path (PATH prepend); DRAM2Utils builds subprocess env explicitly; dram2-env.sh not sourced
decisions.scratch_root: dram2.work_root (default=launch_dir on /scratch1); mkdtemp not TemporaryDirectory; never /tmp
decisions.failure_evidence: keep-on-failure preserves scratch dir + copies pipeline_info/ + .nextflow.log to <work_root>/failed-<run_id>/; dram2.keep_work opt-in for success

acceptance: annotate(proteins, gene_coords, databases=['kofam']) on a real genome with b0001-style locus_ids completes COMBINE_ANNOTATIONS exit 0 and returns records whose gene_id maps back to the original store locus_ids
offline unit test: protein dict with non-prodigal ids (b0001) round-trips (emitted id numeric final token; output remaps to b0001)
documented non-stale way to get NXF_VER=24.10.5 + PATH without sourcing dram2-env.sh
failure path preserves work dir + pipeline_info for debugging
confront_folded: stalls 1-8 + FC4 (scratch subdir) + FC6 (is_available warn); 17 Acceptance Criteria appended to fullprompt.md
